summary: Executed backend API regression and frontend integration/UI checks against the public preview URL. Core pages, routing, filters, structured data, responsiveness, and contact flow pass. AI chatbot widget fails to open due bottom-right click interception.

ui_bugs.issue: AI chat widget cannot be opened; toggle button click is intercepted/overlapped by another bottom-right anchor element.

integration_issues.flow: AI advisor widget open -> disclaimer/prompts/context -> chat send
integration_issues.issue: Blocked at first step because chat panel does not open from UI despite force clicks.

action_items: Fix bottom-right overlap/click interception so AI chat toggle is reliably clickable.
Retest full AI chat flow (open panel, suggested prompts, context fields, POST /api/chat placeholder response rendering).
critical_code_review_comments: Critical UX/integration issue: two fixed bottom-right interactive elements conflict (chat launcher and another floating anchor), causing chat launcher to be non-functional.
All tested backend routes return expected status and data shapes; no API-side blockers detected for required scope.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ contact submissions via /api/contact during regression checks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL used from frontend/.env. Backend pytest suite created and passing. Chat issue reproduced consistently in 3 runs; panel count remained 0 after forced clicks. Diagnostic check showed elementFromPoint over chat button center is an <a> element (non-chat), indicating overlap interception.
rca of the issue: Repro: open home page -> click [data-testid='ai-chat-toggle-button'] (force) -> expect [data-testid='ai-chat-panel']; actual panel absent. Repeated twice in same run, and in dedicated run. Diagnostic JS on button center returned top element tag 'A', not the button, while a separate floating bottom-right badge is visible. Root cause is stacking/position overlap click interception in bottom-right corner. Mitigation: offset one widget, raise chat z-index above external badge, or reserve non-overlapping safe area.
